entities.description: l1 is a limitation of existing works described as the failure to capture the deep connection of document structure and semantics
l2 is a limitation of existing works described as the static nature of query workflows
text based approaches are methods that cannot capture the structural layout of the document
layout segmented methods are approaches that preserve document structure but fail to capture relationships between different blocks
real world qa scenarios are contexts where user queries are highly heterogeneous
static or manually predefined workflows are uniform strategies applied to diverse query needs
question decomposition is a method required for complex queries
document is the object whose structure and semantics are being analyzed in the text
tables are examples of hierarchical blocks nested within a specific section of a document
section is a part of a document where tables may be nested
user queries are inputs in real world qa scenarios that range from simple to complex
keyword lookups are simple types of user queries mentioned in the text
multi hop questions are complex queries requiring evidence synthesis across different document parts
evidence refers to information scattered across different parts of a document needed for multi hop reasoning
hierarchical blocks are structural elements of a document containing relationships
multi hop reasoning is the capability limited by methods that cannot capture relationships between document blocks
overall performance is the metric affected by the limitations of existing methods

relations.description: l1 is caused by text based approaches failing to capture document structure
l2 is caused by the application of static or manually predefined workflows to diverse query needs
text based approaches suffer from the limitation l1
layout segmented methods contribute to the limitation l2 by failing to capture relationships between blocks
real world qa scenarios involve diverse queries that make static or manually predefined workflows inefficient
complex queries often require question decomposition
l1 concerns the failure to capture the deep connection of document structure and semantics
text based approaches analyze the document but fail to capture its structural layout
tables are nested within a specific section of the document
layout segmented methods preserve hierarchical blocks but fail to capture relationships between them
layout segmented methods limit the capability for multi hop reasoning across blocks
user queries are the inputs found within real world qa scenarios
keyword lookups are a type of user query mentioned in the text
multi hop questions are a type of user query mentioned in the text
multi hop questions require synthesizing evidence scattered across the document
applying static workflows to diverse needs affects the overall performance negatively
simple queries do not require question decomposition
